headline: Iran seizes two cargo ships in Strait of Hormuz after attacking three vessels
summary: The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps says it is escorting the seized ships to the Iranian coast. Trump extends US-Iran ceasefire.

headline: Trump buys time for Iran deal after frantic day of diplomacy
summary: The US president's decision marked the second time in as many weeks he has backed off a threat to escalate the war.

headline: EU approves €90bn loan for Ukraine as pipeline reopens ending deadlock
summary: Ukraine reopened the Druzhba pipeline after months of stalemate over stalled oil supplies to Hungary.

headline: Lufthansa cuts 20,000 summer flights as fuel prices surge
summary: The airline is the latest to cut flights as the US-Israel war with Iran sends jet fuel prices soaring.

headline: South Korean fighter jets collided due to pilots taking pictures
summary: One of the pilots has been fined thousands of dollars by the military to cover repair costs.

headline: Taiwan president cancels trip after African countries revoke flight permits
summary: Taiwan has accused Beijing of putting pressure on African countries to close their airspace.

headline: Gunman at Mexican pyramid carried materials related to Columbine massacre
summary: The gunman who fired on tourists at Teotihuacán carried materials related to the 1999 Columbine massacre.

headline: UK passes law banning cigarette sales to anyone born after 2009
summary: Children 17 or younger, and anyone born in the future, will never be able to legally buy cigarettes.

headline: Nepal's Home Affairs Minister resigns over investment questions
summary: Sudan Gurung resigned, the second minister to leave the month-old government.

headline: Canadian PM Mark Carney faces challenge redefining trade with US under Trump
summary: After securing a majority government, Carney's biggest challenge is trade relations with the US.
